paragraphs.text: In this chapter, I would like to say something related to the moment when already we are so well disposed that the prayer becomes really possible and alive. Considering what I have said and what remains understood in essence, the sentence is obviously a relationship, a way to relate to the living God arrives. moment when this relationship transforms into something alive. And placed as it relates to relationship, I want to start with something that refers to similar to the sentence and to human relationships.
A relationship becomes personal and real at the moment it choose one person from the crowd. Then that person it becomes unique in its own right when it ceases to be anonymous.

Someone has talked about 'the corporation' in which, instead of to have first names and last names and qualities and personality, we are defined in general terms as "taxpayers", and thus successively. In our relationships with people, many arise 51

sometimes that element of anonymity 'they'. We speak in the third person persona cuando pensamos que alguien puede ser reemplazado easily by another, because the relationship is functional, impersonal, and that "function" can be performed by anyone, while a "person" it cannot be replaced by any other. One could say that a the relationship becomes real as soon as one starts to think about it persona en términos de «tú», en lugar de «usted». Usted sabe muy well the difference between a relationship based on "you" and "me" with another based on "him" and "me".

The prayer begins when, instead of thinking of a distant God, "He", "The Almighty", etc., one might think in terms of "You", when it is no longer about a third-person relationship, but in the first and second person. Let us take, for example, the Book of Job, where there are problems. Let's take many other cases in the Holy Scriptures and in life, in the lives of the saints and fishermen, where tensions and violent confrontations occur.

There is always something personal. There is no sentence as long as there is one. cautious, distant, and cold relationship, as long as there is protocol between God and we, as long as we cannot speak to him, but address him through through long and complex sequences of words and actions. But there comes a moment when we abandon all ceremony and speak In the first and second person. We say 'I' and expect that 'He' it is "You".

There is another circumstance in a cordial human relationship: that in que buscamos el nombre de una persona. No me refiero a un nombre generally lacking meaning, except in the case where a a certain person has something to do with a name. You all they all know, for example, how personal it is, in a positive aspect and also negative, it can be a nickname. A nickname can be a means of to hurt you, to leave you aside, to destroy any feeling between two people; but it can also be a name that is only two. people use, or a small group of people who are so deeply and intimately united with one another, that the name results full of meaning for them, because it is extremely personal. As much the more absurd it is, in a way, the more personal it is, because no one I would inventory, except for you.
Then there is the last name. The last name often seems strange to us, a a general term like 'humanity'; so many people have the same 52

surname! However, if we look at it more closely in context from human relationships, we can realize that the surname it is the brand of a community. From generation to generation, towards back, in history, people who are of our blood, whose life is inside our bones, forming part of our heritage, in our own mind has carried this name, and this name unites us to very distant generations of people and probably will unite us, in the future, to others; through the different ties of marriages and families We will establish a vast network of closely related people. among themselves. If instead of thinking about last names, you think about inheritance, In genealogy, isn't that what we find in two of the Gospels regarding the Lord? Is this not precisely what this genealogy indicates: a connection from generation to generation between concrete and authentic human beings? So a surname is something what we can address with immense interest because it supports all our past in one word, and if we think about other people in these , surnames could come to life. Instead of being the expression of the individuality of some person and their own individuality in relationship with us, like a nickname, would unite us all. strike this unique person, a complete set of beings...

Then there is the Christian name, the name we receive in the baptism: it is the name by which God takes possession of a the name of baptism connects the person with God, because upon receiving it, he dies with Christ and is reborn; but it also unites him to a variety of people who have been given the same name and, first of all, with the one who converted a pagan name into Cristiano, the first saint who brought him to the Church.
We also have another name, one that we do not know.

You remember the passage in the Book of Revelation that says that in In the Kingdom, each one will receive a white stone with a name written on it. above, a name known only by God and by whom it receive. This is not a nickname, nor a surname, nor a proper name. It is a name, a word that is identical to us, that coincides with we, what is we. We can almost say that it is a word that God pronounced when He wished our existence and that it is us, as we are her. This name defines our absolute and irreplicable individuality regarding God. No one can knowing the name, nor, in the final extreme, can one know 53
no one knows him like God does; and yet, thanks to this Name can know everything that refers to us.

You may ask why I focus on names. It is because part of our prayer relates directly to God and it is our bond with Him; but also another part of our prayer is the bond that connects us with the outside world, and when we pray one for another, we bring names to God and nothing more. But those names are full of meaning, according to the circumstances, according to whether we are capable or not of capturing the depth of what we say. If we mention people before God without no sense of the name, just using names like labels, lacking meaning, our relationship is of poor quality; if we pronounce a name with one of the meanings that I have I briefly deal with explaining, so our prayer does not it's just like presenting a person in our hands, but rather connects us to this person with a relationship not of compassion, not of love, but of identification, of sharing, of solidarity, that it has different characteristics.

This is also true in the other direction. Unless we find the true name of God, we will not have free, real, joyful and open access to Him. As long as we have to address God with common formulas, such as 'The Almighty', 'The Lord God' as long as we have to put the article 'the' in front of the word to make it anonymous, to make it a generic term, we cannot use it as a personal name. But there are times when the sacred writers, for example, start with something that has the characteristics of a nickname, something that nobody else could have said, what is on the edge of the possible and the impossible, which is only possible because there is a friendship. Remember the psalm; after some more measured forms of expression, suddenly David "You, my Joy!" At that moment, the entire psalm is filled with life. Saying: "Oh, You, our Lord", "Oh, You, the Almighty", and similar things, things are said to God about Him; but letting oneself carried by the feeling and saying: 'Oh, You, my Joy', the matter change. And when can we say to God: "Oh, You, my Joy", or when you can say to her: "Oh. You, Pain of my life, oh, You, that you are in the middle of her like a torment, like a problem, like an obstacle!" when we can turn to Him with violence, so we have established a relationship of prayer. 54

For this reason, it is very important for us to look for names in our experience that can be applied to God. In principle, because we can change our name from time to time. There is moments when we perceive an aspect of our relationship with God and at other times we see other aspects, in the same way. that, in friendly or affectionate relationships between our friends, not we use not just one expression to speak to each other, but a variety of shades and tones. To deal with God, we have 'The Almighty', we have 'The Lord', we have 'The Creator', we have 'The "Providence", we have "Wisdom", but we also have a a very simple name, like Jesus, which is, I will say, a name Cristiano.

It may sound strange to say that Christ has a Christian name; I hope that will understand what I mean. It reminds me of a discussion that one of my parishioners, who is Christian, argued with her husband, who does not It is. He spent forty years of his life trying to prove to her. that Christianity is not worth it, and one day, desperate, she said: How can you say that, when God was first a Jew and then... he became Christian?" The way I say that Jesus is a name Cristiano can make you remember this event from the primitive times; however, it is a human name, the first Christian name written in the annals of the Church. And if we remember this, if we are aware of the ties it establishes between Him and we will understand why generations of Christians have clinging to this name; probably not because Saint Paul says: At the name of Jesus, every knee shall bow, because Although this is true, it is not this that inspires love in a name.
This may correspond to the mention of 'The Almighty' or 'The' "Lord," but the name of Jesus is a living, real name, a name personal.

And you can find many other names. I am sure that if one day "Oh, You, my Joy" or any exclamation of this kind it leaves his lips, it will be the moment when you will have discovered a relationship between Him and you that is your own, which is a relationship that do not share with other people. I do not mean that they should not share it. We have words for God that belong to everyone us, but there are words that belong only to me or you, in the same way that, in human relationships, there are surnames, 55

There are proper names, there are nicknames. It's good that you have a nickname. to call upon Almighty God, a nickname in which it is condensed all the depth of your heart, all the warmth of what you are capable; it's like a way of saying: 'I, personally, interpret in this way your personality.

If in the process of discovering where you stand in your relationship with God -- to what extent is a stranger -- do you reach the moment of knock on the door, to descend more and more into one's own intimacy, address the prayer to oneself, approach the point where there is a door to knock on, to the point where it can be opened, will arrive a the moment when the door will open, but then you must have a name for God. You must tell Him a word that demonstrates It is you whom I was looking for and not a human being. undefined in search of an anonymous God.

In the process of searching, you will have suffered sorrow, anguish, hope, trust, the whole range of human emotions. God He will have been the Desired and he will have been the Frustrating. He will have been That One for whom you sigh and the one you hate because he slips away, The one whom you love above all things, without whom you do not can live and to whom cannot forgive, because they do not answer, and many other things. And throughout this search many will arise gradualmente palabras, con las que podrá hablar con Dios por su personal experience in the search for the Holy Grail, words that will be your particulars. You may notice that they coincide with many words that others have used. Then they will cease to be anonymous words, they will be words that you will have in common with other people, but that will they have truly turned into their own words. But do not use words that are in a common dictionary, words that are not belong. When you start to hear a chain ringing on the other side of the door, when I feel that it is going to open, then pronounce the proper names and call God by the name with which distinguishes it in his own life. At that moment they will have met.

In the relationship of eternal dependency and enrichment that follows, you will have plenty of time to discover other words, to discard the words of hate and anguish. Like the martyrs that are spoken of in the Book of Revelation, you will say: 'You have been just and verdadero en todo momento». Y estas palabras borrarán todas las words of bitterness, all the names of a cruel tone, but you 56
will retain the personal names created by you, which will be a a real relationship and an authentic way to communicate with the living God.

What I have said regarding the learning of prayer is, I believe, quite practical for you to be able to exercise. It is obvious that much more could have been said about other things, but I tried to to experiment around what I have suggested and you will see that it is not a waste for a while. Look for a name, and if it doesn't have a name, I don't know. surprise that no one hears him: he calls no one. 57
